videos.title: [지금뉴스] 삼성전자 총파업 D-6…30일 파업 막는 ‘긴급조정권’이란? / KBS 2026.05.15.
videos.subtitle_summary:
1. Samsung Electronics is threatening a general strike on the 21st, and there's discussion about the possibility of the government using emergency powers to prevent it.
2. The Minister of Trade, Industry and Energy, Kim Jung-han, stated that semiconductors are the only growth engine for our country's strategic assets and as a minister, he believes that if a strike occurs, emergency measures will be necessary.
3. Emergency powers can be activated by the Minister of Labor and Employment when there is a risk of significant harm to the national economy or daily life. If activated, the union must stop all strikes for 30 days and the government will intervene in mediation and regulation. However, actual cases are rare, with only a few instances such as the Korean National Construction Corporation in 1969, Hyundai Motor Company in 1993, Asiana Airlines and Korean Air flight attendants in 2005. The most recent case was also after the strike began, and there has been no precedent of emergency powers being activated before a strike. Samsung Electronics is reportedly preparing for a general strike by reducing semiconductor production and implementing emergency measures. If implemented, the potential damage could reach up to 100 trillion won due to the nature of 24-hour semiconductor production lines where even a brief shutdown would result in significant losses. However, the Ministry of Labor and Employment is currently not reviewing the use of emergency powers, instead prioritizing negotiations for resolution. The Central Reconciliation and mediation Committee has requested a post-strike settlement meeting on the 16th before the Samsung strike occurs. The union has stated that management must first present concrete details on key issues such as abolishing performance-based bonuses, institutionalization, and transparency before they are willing to negotiate.

videos.title: 삼성전자 노조 “파업 이후 협의”…파업 D-6, 강 대 강 대치 / KBS  2026.05.15.
videos.subtitle_summary:
1. Samsung Electronics union has announced that the strike, which was scheduled to start in 6 days, will proceed as planned. They have not agreed to any further negotiations regarding additional adjustments or a higher wage increase.
2. The union emphasized that they will carry out the strike as planned on June 7th and beyond. The management has expressed concerns about the national economy and the public's worries, and has requested discussions with the union.
3. Despite the ongoing tense situation, no significant changes have been observed in response to the union's demands for a wage hike and transparency. The Labor Minister Kim Young-hun has emphasized the need for dialogue to resolve the issue.

videos.title: 삼성전자 노사 협상 교착…파업 강행 가능성 / KBS  2026.05.15.
videos.subtitle_summary:
1. Samsung Electronics proposed a plan for transparency in performance-based pay, but the union is considering discussions only from June 7th onwards, potentially leading to a strike starting May 21st.
2. The company's management has expressed a willingness to engage in open dialogue without conditions, following the union's rejection of post-retirement adjustments discussed at a Central Labor Welfare Committee meeting.
3. The union has publicly disclosed parts of the minutes from the post-retirement adjustment meeting held on May 11th, which may have contributed to the rejection of post-retirement adjustment limitations.
